title: Weights are adjustable knobs
summary: A model learns by changing numbers that shape how inputs become outputs.
body_html: A weight is a learned number inside a model. During training, the system compares a prediction with the target, computes a loss, and nudges many weights so future predictions move in a better direction. The idea is simple; the scale is what makes modern models surprising.
key_points: Weights are learned parameters, not hand-written rules.
Training adjusts weights to reduce loss on examples.
Generalization is the test: useful behavior on cases not memorized.

exercise.prompt: A classifier has only seen training examples with red labels. What is the safest prediction about a new blue example?
choices: It will certainly classify blue correctly because training reduced loss.
It may fail because training success does not prove generalization to unseen cases.
It cannot make any prediction unless blue appears in the prompt.
exercise.explain: Lower training loss is not the same as tested generalization.

outcome: You can distinguish learned parameters from hand-coded rules.
